title: Muir Woods San Francisco.  WORTH it?  Visiting Muir Woods National Monument Park...#muirwoods
content:
Muir Woods San Francisco.  WORTH it?  Visiting Muir Woods National Monument Park...#muirwoods
that place

26 Feb 2024
Muir Woods National Monument is just north of San Francisco Golden Gate Bridge and is one of the few areas California hiking areas that preserved massive "old growth" redwood trees.  The Muir Woods park is accessible for old and young alike so the main trails can get busy with hikers, walkers and strollers.  We show you the best ways to get to Muir Woods and get around the park.  

We show you the transportation options and give multiple tips on experiencing Muir Woods along with some redwood tree information. We show you several hiking trails and  take a 2 mile loop hike along redwood creek including hillside trail experiencing Founders Grove, Cathedral grove, centennial tree and much more.  We show you the Canopy view trail and Fern Creek trail that lead to Panoramic Hwy and a special secret across from Mountain house inn.  

This may not be the best park for forest meditation (Also known as Forest Bathing) but the beauty and majestic feel are awesome.  We give some local tips on serene redwood tree forest bathing in San Francisco.

title: Is Muir Woods Worth Visiting? - Travel Caffeine
content: # Is Muir Woods Worth Visiting? Muir Woods National Monument is a 550 acre park filled with old growth coastal redwoods; a humbling place just outside of San Francisco, California in Golden Gate National Recreation Area. If you’ve never seen redwood and sequoia trees, it’s an absolute must-do on any California trip. As a big fan of these trees and huge advocate of the National Parks, you might think the conclusion of the question posted by my post title is foregone, that it’s a rhetorical question, or that this post is going to extol the virtues of Muir Woods. For at least some tourists to California, I think Muir Woods might not be worth it. If your trip to California is taking you only to the Bay Area, Muir Woods is a must. Sequoia National Park is located south east of San Francisco, and is easy to hit if you’re also doing Yosemite National Park (and you absolutely should).

title: A Fun Day Trip to Muir Woods from San Francisco | Travel Guide
content: # A Fun Day Trip to Muir Woods from San Francisco. Muir Woods National Monument is a grove of ancient coastal redwoods in Mill Valley, over the Golden Gate Bridge from San Francisco. A day trip to Muir Woods from San Francisco is a popular activity, and well worth the trip during your time in the Bay Area. Here are my tips for doing a day trip, including stops to see some great views on the way back to the city. 1. Taking a day trip to Muir Woods and Golden Gate Bridge Lookouts from San Francisco. While many choose not to hire a car in San Francisco (public transport is great), this is a fantastic day trip, and could easily be worked into a bigger road trip up the coast to Seattle or the Cascades. The drive out over the Golden Gate Bridge is fantastic. It is imperative, if you are driving, that you book your parking for Muir Woods.
